system.description:
Historical Curiosity

The famous cartographer Naladicha died, and his ghost was woven into a spirit anchor connected to a pen on a wire. The drooping pen scribbled nonstop, dipping to indicate a page turn. Two books were filled with scribbles before the pen stilled. These lines and shapes may be maps of the Ghost Field.

One consulting Whisper reported that when she attuned to the books using an expensive and difficult ritual, the maps became luminous and four dimensional, revealing lost secrets in Duskvol.
